user_message: Can you extract the forecast prep from this support ticket for Lucerne Publishing? The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-03-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (Lucerne Publishing)

[00:01:15] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is forecast prep.

[00:06:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] David (Lucerne Publishing): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:16:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:18:00] David (Lucerne Publishing): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:25:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:28:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-N trap (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-N failure mode (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-N -- Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
